title: Joint agency EEG dataset
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous EEG recordings (hyperscanning) and behavioral data from 32 participant pairs engaged in a joint musical action task. Participants performed either musical duets or constant pitch sequences while their sense of joint agency was assessed via self-report ratings. The dataset includes raw EEG data from 64 channels (32 per participant) in BrainVision format, synchronized behavioral measures of performance synchronization, and detailed event markers for experimental conditions and trial structure.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired simultaneously from two participants using 32-channel systems per participant (64 channels total) in BrainVision format. Participants performed 8 tone sequences (4 musical duets and 4 constant pitch sequences) with 5 joint trials per sequence, yielding 40 test trials per pair. Behavioral synchronization performance was calculated as the mean absolute asynchrony between participants' note onsets normalized by inter-onset interval, with standard deviation computed across all beats per trial. Joint agency was assessed using a 1-7 self-report rating scale.
